page: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring an AMD EPYC processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 1 shared vCPU consisting of 1 physical core and 1 thread, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware profile includes 2.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration available. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AES cryptography, and SHA extensions.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities due to the single shared vCPU configuration. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks, placing in the top 10%, alongside strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. However, multi-core CPU performance and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Memory bandwidth is mixed, with read-only bandwidth categorized as weak, while large block write bandwidth reaches average levels.

Given its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, this server represents a highly cost-efficient entry point for lightweight workloads that benefit from strong bursty single-threaded performance but do not require sustained multi-core execution. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are relatively weak, indicating that the server is poorly suited for high-traffic databases or intensive web serving. It is best utilized for basic general-purpose tasks, lightweight background scripts, single-threaded utility processing, or low-demand development environments.
description: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server equipped with 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this server delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, its shared CPU allocation and single-core limitation result in weak multi-core performance, low memory bandwidth, and poor database operation throughput. This resource profile offers a cost-efficient solution for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, utility scripting, and basic development environments, while remaining poorly suited for high-traffic web serving or intensive database applications.
og_description: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is a High Performance virtual server featuring 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-1c-2gb-amd is an x86_64 virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2 GB RAM, and 50 GB NVMe SSD storage for entry-level workloads.
tagline: An entry-level virtual server combining strong single-threaded AMD EPYC performance with local NVMe storage for budget-conscious, lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU running at 2.0 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression
Weak multi-core performance and low memory bandwidth due to shared single-core design
Best suited for lightweight utility scripts, basic development, and single-threaded tasks
